source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the audit policy covers key security concerns

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure that the audit policy covers key security concerns
input_row.description: Ensure	that	the	audit	policy	created	for	the	cluster	covers	key	security	concerns.
input_row.rationale: Security	audit	logs	should	cover	access	and	modification	of	key	resources	in	the	cluster,	to enable	them	to	form	an	effective	part	of	a	security	environment. Impact: Increasing	audit	logging	will	consume	resources	on	the	nodes	or	other	log	destination.
input_row.audit: Review	the	audit	policy	provided	for	the	cluster	and	ensure	that	it	covers	at	least	the following	areas	:- • Access	to	Secrets	managed	by	the	cluster.	Care	should	be	taken	to	only	log	Metadata for	requests	to	Secrets,	ConfigMaps,	and	TokenReviews,	in	order	to	avoid	the	risk	of logging	sensitive	data. • Modification	of	pod	and	deployment	objects. • Use	of	pods/exec,	pods/portforward,	pods/proxy	and	services/proxy. For	most	requests,	minimally	logging	at	the	Metadata	level	is	recommended	(the	most	basic level	of	logging).
input_row.remediation: Consider	modification	of	the	audit	policy	in	use	on	the	cluster	to	include	these	items,	at	a minimum. Default	Value: By	default	Kubernetes	clusters	do	not	log	audit	information. References: 158	|	P a g e 1. https://github.com/k8scop/k8s-security- dashboard/blob/master/configs/kubernetes/adv-audit.yaml 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/debug-application-cluster/audit/#audit-policy 3. https://github.com/falcosecurity/falco/blob/master/examples/k8s_audit_config/a udit-policy.yaml 4. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/cluster/gce/gci/configur e-helper.sh#L735 5. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/91406.htm Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14.6	Enforce	Detailed	Audit	Logging	For	Sensitive	Information Enforce	detailed	audit	logging	for	access	to	nonpublic	data	and	special	authentication	for sensitive	data. Version	7 14.9	Enforce	Detail	Logging	for	Access	or	Changes	to	Sensitive	Data Enforce	detailed	audit	logging	for	access	to	sensitive	data	or	changes	to	sensitive	data (utilizing	tools	such	as	File	Integrity	Monitoring	or	Security	Information	and	Event Monitoring). 4 Worker Nodes This	section	consists	of	security	recommendations	for	the	components	that	run	on Kubernetes	worker	nodes. Note	that	these	components	may	also	run	on	Kubernetes	master	nodes,	so	the recommendations	in	this	section	should	be	applied	to	master	nodes	as	well	as	worker nodes	where	the	master	nodes	make	use	of	these	components. 159	|	P a g e 4.1 Worker Node Configuration Files This	section	covers	recommendations	for	configuration	files	on	the	worker	nodes. To	Perform	an	Automated	Audit	utilizing	CIS-CAT	the	following	parameters	must	be	set	on each	node	being	evaluated. $kubelet_service_config $kubelet_config $kubelet_config_yaml If	you	are	auditing	a	kubeadm	environment	the	default	settings	for	these	values	are	below: export kubelet_service_config=/etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/10- kubeadm.conf export kubelet_config=/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf export kubelet_config_yaml=/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml
input_row.impact: Increasing	audit	logging	will	consume	resources	on	the	nodes	or	other	log	destination.
input_row.references: 158	|	P a g e 1. https://github.com/k8scop/k8s-security- dashboard/blob/master/configs/kubernetes/adv-audit.yaml 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/debug-application-cluster/audit/#audit-policy 3. https://github.com/falcosecurity/falco/blob/master/examples/k8s_audit_config/a udit-policy.yaml 4. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/cluster/gce/gci/configur e-helper.sh#L735 5. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/91406.htm Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14.6	Enforce	Detailed	Audit	Logging	For	Sensitive	Information Enforce	detailed	audit	logging	for	access	to	nonpublic	data	and	special	authentication	for sensitive	data. Version	7 14.9	Enforce	Detail	Logging	for	Access	or	Changes	to	Sensitive	Data Enforce	detailed	audit	logging	for	access	to	sensitive	data	or	changes	to	sensitive	data (utilizing	tools	such	as	File	Integrity	Monitoring	or	Security	Information	and	Event Monitoring). 4 Worker Nodes This	section	consists	of	security	recommendations	for	the	components	that	run	on Kubernetes	worker	nodes. Note	that	these	components	may	also	run	on	Kubernetes	master	nodes,	so	the recommendations	in	this	section	should	be	applied	to	master	nodes	as	well	as	worker nodes	where	the	master	nodes	make	use	of	these	components. 159	|	P a g e 4.1 Worker Node Configuration Files This	section	covers	recommendations	for	configuration	files	on	the	worker	nodes. To	Perform	an	Automated	Audit	utilizing	CIS-CAT	the	following	parameters	must	be	set	on each	node	being	evaluated. $kubelet_service_config $kubelet_config $kubelet_config_yaml If	you	are	auditing	a	kubeadm	environment	the	default	settings	for	these	values	are	below: export kubelet_service_config=/etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/10- kubeadm.conf export kubelet_config=/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf export kubelet_config_yaml=/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml
input_row.justification: While some aspects of Kubernetes cluster configuration can be queried via Alibaba Cloud APIs or CLI (such as cluster details), the specific content and coverage of the audit policy—especially the nuanced evaluation of whether it addresses key security concerns (e.g., logging access to Secrets, ConfigMaps, TokenReviews, and specific pod operations at the correct granularity)—requires human judgment and direct inspection of the policy file. Alibaba Cloud APIs do not expose the full audit policy content or its detailed rules for ACK clusters, nor do they provide a way to programmatically assess the qualitative adequacy of those rules. Therefore, a manual review is necessary to ensure compliance.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. **Access the Alibaba Cloud Console:** - Log in to the Alibaba Cloud Console. - Navigate to "Container Service for Kubernetes" (ACK). - Select the target Kubernetes cluster. 2. **Locate the Audit Policy Configuration:** - Access the cluster’s configuration section. - Identify and download the audit policy file in use (typically `audit-policy.yaml` or similar). - If the audit policy is not visible in the console, access the master node (if permitted) or request the policy from the cluster administrator. 3. **Review Audit Policy Content:** - Open the audit policy file. - Verify that it includes rules for: - Logging access to Secrets, ConfigMaps, and TokenReviews (ensure only metadata is logged for sensitive resources). - Logging modification events for pods and deployments. - Logging use of pods/exec, pods/portforward, pods/proxy, and services/proxy. - Ensure the logging level is set to at least "Metadata" for sensitive resources to avoid exposure of sensitive data. 4. **Confirm Compliance:** - The policy must explicitly cover all key areas listed above. - Document the presence and adequacy of each required rule. - If any area is missing or insufficiently covered, mark as non-compliant.
input_row.default_value: By default Kubernetes clusters do not log audit information.
input_row.additional_information: This control cannot be audited and modified in ACK managed clusters.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- An automated approach is not suitable for this control because the specific content and coverage of the audit policy cannot be queried via Alibaba Cloud APIs, Aliyun CLI, or any commands. A manual review is necessary, as it involves human judgment to evaluate the adequacy of audit policy coverage regarding logging key security concerns in the Kubernetes environment.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access the Alibaba Cloud Console:**
   - Log in to the Alibaba Cloud Console (https://home.console.aliyun.com).
   - Navigate to "Container Service for Kubernetes" (ACK).
   - Select the target Kubernetes cluster from the list available.

2. **Locate the Audit Policy Configuration:**
   - In the cluster's settings, find the configuration section related to auditing.
   - Identify and download the audit policy file being used (typically named `audit-policy.yaml` or similar).
   - If the audit policy is not visible in the console, access the master node (if permissions allow) via SSH or request the audit policy from the cluster administrator.

3. **Review Audit Policy Content:**
   - Open the downloaded audit policy file using a text editor.
   - Verify that it includes rules for:
     - Logging access to Secrets and ConfigMaps, ensuring only metadata is logged to prevent exposure of sensitive data.
     - Logging modification events for pod and deployment objects.
     - Logging operations such as pods/exec, pods/portforward, pods/proxy, and services/proxy.
     - The logging level for sensitive resources must be set to at least "Metadata".

4. **Confirm Compliance:**
   - Document the presence of each required rule and evaluate its adequacy to ensure it covers all key areas as specified.
   - If any of the required sections are missing or inadequately covered, mark the cluster as non-compliant and note specific issues for remediation.
